bids.reasoning: At the beginning of the negotiation, I prioritize my maximum utility without the pressure of imminent deadlines. The opponent's offer, consisting entirely of "valueE" across all issues, registered as the lowest utility for me due to its value preference of 0.20. Since my higher priorities are issueA and issueB, I will choose "valueA" for these, the top-valued preferences for all issues, enhancing my satisfaction.

I am targeting a high utility offer initially to establish my position, as it is early in the negotiation process (t=0.02). The opponent's offers so far have been at the lowest utility for me, showing no movement from their initial position. Given the time is still early, I will continue to propose an offer that maintains my utilities high with values for each issue that have a utility of 1.00.

At this early stage of negotiation (t=0.04), I am holding firm on my initial offer because there is ample time remaining. The opponent has consistently offered the lowest utility values from my perspective. This indicates they are holding out their positions without wavering. I aim to maximize my utility score and will continue to propose a high-utility offer, especially since there have been no concessions from the opponent thus far.

At this point in the negotiation, the opponent has only slightly altered their offer by changing the value of issueA from valueE to valueD, which suggests limited flexibility and possible anchoring to a low utility offer. Given the time is still early (t=0.06), I aim to maintain higher utility from my perspective by proposing a negotiation that slightly sacrifices on less important issues like issueD and issueE but maintain high utility on crucial issues like issueA, issueB, and issueC. This strategy provides room for future concessions while preserving most of my utility at this phase.

The opponent has shown some minor concession on issueA by offering valueD, while consistently sticking to their least-preferred choices on other issues. Time is still early, so maintaining high value across other issues makes sense to push my preferences. This approach balances my utility needs with the necessity to encourage reciprocal concessions from the opponent.
